content.verdict: St. Regis Amman sets the gold standard for luxury in Jordan, with legendary St Regis butler service in every room, the country's finest Iridium Spa and walking-distance access to Abdali Boulevard. It is the obvious choice for couples who want Manhattan-level polish before heading out to Petra or the desert.
content.best_room: The Caroline Astor Suite is Amman's most romantic address, with 110 square metres of living space, a freestanding soaking tub, Bulgari amenities and a butler who will unpack, press and draw bespoke baths. For a lighter splurge, the Deluxe Room with Citadel view is still served by the same 24-hour butler.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and butler welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Your St Regis butler meets you at Queen Alia airport, handles luggage and chills champagne in the suite. Dine at Sukar for modern Levantine small plates before evening turndown.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Roman Amman on foot
itinerary_7_nights.description: The butler arranges a private guide for the Citadel, Roman Theatre and Jordan Museum, followed by lunch at Hashem in the old downtown and afternoon tea with harp music at the St Regis Library.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Iridium Spa couples day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend the day in the 2,200 square metre Iridium Spa with a traditional hammam scrub, couples aromatherapy massage and a long swim in the indoor pool. Dinner at La Capitale brasserie in the hotel.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Jerash and Ajloun
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private car north to Jerash's Roman colonnades and Ajloun Castle on its wooded hilltop. Picnic hamper prepared by the hotel kitchen before return for sunset cocktails at the St Regis Bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Dead Sea day spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Chauffeured descent to the Dead Sea for floating, mud treatments and lunch at a beach club. Return to Amman for a sabrage champagne ritual at 6pm in the King Cole-inspired bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Wine, art and rooftop dining
itinerary_7_nights.description: Visit Darat al Funun for Arab contemporary art, the gold souk for matching wedding bands if you did not exchange them yet, and dine on the rooftop with Amman's skyline glittering.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final Middle Eastern breakfast in your suite, butler-packed bags and transfer to the airport or a private driver onward to Petra for the next leg of your honeymoon.

caveats: The rooftop pool is beautiful but small, and crowded weddings in the ballroom can briefly animate the lobby — ask the butler to route you via the private club elevator to avoid any bustle.
Amman is a conservative Arab capital, so bikinis are fine at the pool but not elsewhere, and alcohol is served only inside the hotel during Ramadan and on public holidays.
Room categories vary widely in size from 45 to 260 square metres, so couples should specifically book a Deluxe King or higher to guarantee the suite-like St Regis experience in photographs.

faqs.question: Is this hotel good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — it is the most romantic address in Jordan outside of Wadi Rum. The butler service, Iridium Spa and afternoon champagne sabrage make it ideal for couples celebrating a special occasion.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: April to early June and late September to November deliver sunny 25C days and cool evenings perfect for rooftop dining. July and August are hotter but the pool and spa compensate nicely.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Request the same butler throughout the stay, reserve the Iridium hammam for two, book a table at La Capitale's chef's counter and arrange the Petra overnight package through the concierge.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, families are welcome, but the property feels noticeably adult and business-led, especially midweek. Weekend brunches attract local families, so choose weekday stays if you want maximum calm.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: The Caroline Astor Suite is the pinnacle, with a tub big enough for two and panoramic Abdali views. Deluxe King rooms are smaller but still include the signature butler and marble bathroom.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Queen Alia International Airport (AMM). The St Regis is 32 kilometres north in the new Abdali district, and the hotel offers a BMW 7 Series transfer taking around 40 minutes.
